title: Expose lifecycle mutations through MCP
description: Add typed MCP tools for the finalized task and issue lifecycle operations and shared-root behavior as thin wrappers over core functions.
acceptance: MCP exposes task create, update, set-status, and reopen tools plus issue list, show, create, update, and close tools.
Tool schemas reuse or faithfully map the core Zod mutation inputs and return CommandResult-compatible structured content.
Server startup resolves and discloses the selected ledger root without contaminating stdio protocol output.
MCP tests invoke every new tool across success and representative coded failure paths.
The MCP layer never writes canonical JSON directly.
notes: Keep stdio protocol output clean; root disclosure belongs in structured metadata or stderr diagnostics.
